sections.paragraph: m. pl. Ch. the friends or the ministers of the king, Staatsräthe, viziers. Dan 3:24 4:33 6:8 , and הַדָּֽבְרֵי מַלְכָּא 3:27 , “the king’s highest friends.” As to the etymology, I can scarcely doubt but that this is the Chald. דָּֽבְרִין leaders, governors, with the Hebrew article prefixed, which coalesces into one word, just as the Arabic article does with some Hebr. words; [“So Lee.”] see אַל p. 45. A . Formerly, from the syllable בר (by comparison with גִּזְבָּר, דְּתָבָר) I conjectured this word to be of Persic origin, like the other official names in these chapters, but as to what הַד might mean, it had to be left undetermined.
